rationale: In the model formulation, the authors explicitly cite Andrews et al. (2002) to justify using a latent-class (discrete) heterogeneity specification. The citation is linked to a concrete methodological choice by claiming empirical equivalence between discrete and continuous heterogeneity approaches. This is more than a passing mention because it supports how the core model is specified for the paper's main analyses.
relation_description: The citing paper uses the cited paper as methodological support for adopting a discrete latent-class representation of consumer heterogeneity instead of a continuous HB approach. The cited study underpins this modeling choice by providing prior empirical evidence that the two representations can perform similarly.
